name: Freshdesk API
what_it_does: Customer support and helpdesk platform API for managing tickets, contacts, agents, knowledge bases, and automation rules. Part of Freshworks suite, competing with Zendesk.
use_cases: Automating ticket creation from monitoring alerts and incident systems
AI-powered ticket classification, routing, and prioritization
Syncing customer data between CRM and support platform
Building agent-assisted support with automated response suggestions
not_for: Live chat and messaging-first support (use Intercom instead)
Enterprise-grade complex workflows at very large scale
Real-time call center operations (limited voice integration)
best_when: An agent needs to create, manage, or automate helpdesk tickets in Freshdesk, especially for incident management or customer support workflow automation.
avoid_when: Your team uses Zendesk (different API) or needs messaging-first customer engagement.

auth.notes: API key used as username in HTTP Basic Auth with any password (typically 'X'). Or pass API key directly in header. Simple and straightforward for agent use — no OAuth flow needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: unlimited agents, basic ticketing, email support only
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for small teams. API access available on all plans. Per-agent pricing can be expensive for large support teams. Competitive with Zendesk on features per dollar.

performance.rate_limits: 400-500 requests per minute depending on plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include 'errors' array with field, message, and code. HTTP status codes are standard. Documentation is comprehensive. Some API behaviors are underdocumented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Duplicate ticket creation is possible. Agents should search for existing tickets before creating to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits vary by plan — Free plan has much lower limits than paid
Freshdesk subdomain is part of API URL (yourcompany.freshdesk.com) — must be configured per account
Ticket status codes are numeric (1=Open, 2=Pending, 3=Resolved, 4=Closed) — not human-readable in requests
Custom fields must be pre-created in Freshdesk before they can be set via API
Bulk operations have separate endpoints with different rate limits
API v2 is current — v1 is deprecated but some examples in the wild still use it
